{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c1f3d20316074819a4a34eb7","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Pytest Command Pattern for Detailed Assertion Output\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tThis document provides the definitive pytest command pattern for producing detailed assertion output with line numbers, expected values, and actual values.\n6\t\n7\t## Optimal Command Pattern\n8\t\n9\t### For Single Tests\n10\t\n11\t```bash\n12\tpytest <test_file>::<test_name> -vv --tb=short\n13\t```\n14\t\n15\t### For Entire Test Files\n16\t\n17\t```bash\n18\tpytest <test_file> -vv --tb=short\n19\t```\n20\t\n21\t### For Running Only Failed Tests from Last Run\n22\t\n23\t```bash\n24\tpytest <test_file> -vv --tb=short --lf\n25\t```\n26\t\n27\t### For All Tests with Maximum Detail\n28\t\n29\t```bash\n30\tpytest -vv --tb=short\n31\t```\n32\t\n33\t## Flag Breakdown\n34\t\n35\t| Flag | Purpose | Effect |\n36\t|------|---------|--------|\n37\t| `-vv` | Double verbose | Provides maximum detail in assertion output including character-level diffs |\n38\t| `--tb=short` | Short traceback format | Shows line numbers, assertion details, and diffs in a readable format |\n39\t| `--lf` | Last failed | Rerun only the tests that failed in the last run |\n40\t\n41\t## Why This Combination Works\n42\t\n43\tThe `-vv --tb=short` combination provides:\n44\t\n45\t1. **Exact Line Numbers**: Shows precise file and line location of assertion failures\n46\t   ```\n47\t   test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:22: in test_simple_equality\n48\t   ```\n49\t\n50\t2. **Expected vs Actual Values**: Clear display of what was compared\n51\t   ```\n52\t   assert 'hello there' == 'hello world'\n53\t   ```\n54\t\n55\t3. **Detailed Visual Diffs**: Character-by-character precision with indicators\n56\t   ```\n57\t   - hello world\n58\t   + hello there\n59\t       ^^^^   ^^^^\n60\t   ```\n61\t\n62\t4. **Index Information**: For lists, shows which index differs\n63\t   ```\n64\t   At index 2 diff: 0 != 3\n65\t   ```\n66\t\n67\t5. **Structured Diffs**: Hierarchical view for nested data structures\n68\t   ```\n69\t   user.address.city: Expected 'New York' but got 'San Francisco'\n70\t   ```\n71\t\n72\t## Environment-Specific Considerations\n73\t\n74\t### NixOS (ARMOR Project)\n75\t\n76\t```bash\n77\tnix-shell -p python312Packages.pytest --run \"pytest <test_file>::<test_name> -vv --tb=short\"\n78\t```\n79\t\n80\t### Standard Python Environments\n81\t\n82\t```bash\n83\tpytest <test_file>::<test_name> -vv --tb=short\n84\t```\n85\t\n86\t### With Virtual Environments\n87\t\n88\t```bash\n89\tsource venv/bin/activate\n90\tpytest <test_file>::<test_name> -vv --tb=short\n91\t```\n92\t\n93\t### With pyproject.toml Configuration\n94\t\n95\tIf you have pytest configured in `pyproject.toml`:\n96\t\n97\t```toml\n98\t[tool.pytest.ini_options]\n99\taddopts = \"-vv --tb=short\"\n100\t```\n101\t\n102\tThen you can run:\n103\t```bash\n104\tpytest <test_file>::<test_name>\n105\t```\n106\t\n107\t## Alternative Flag Combinations\n108\t\n109\t### Maximum Detail (with local variables)\n110\t\n111\t```bash\n112\tpytest -vv --tb=short -l\n113\t```\n114\t\n115\tThe `-l` flag shows local variables in tracebacks for additional debugging context.\n116\t\n117\t### Long Tracebacks (full stack)\n118\t\n119\t```bash\n120\tpytest -vv --tb=long\n121\t```\n122\t\n123\tUse this when you need the full stack trace, not just the failure location.\n124\t\n125\t### Machine-Readable Output\n126\t\n127\t```bash\n128\tpytest -vv --tb=short there' == 'hello world'\n149\tE       - hello world\n150\tE       ?    ---  ^^^\n151\tE       + hello there\n152\tE       ?        +++ ^^^^\n153\t```\n154\t\n155\t### Dictionary test_example.py:39: in test_dict_equality\n165\t    assert {'name': 'test', 'value': 42} == {'name': 'test', 'value': 100}\n166\tE     AssertionError: assert {'name': 'test', 'value': 42} == {'name': 'test', 'value': 100}\n167\tE       Omitting 1 identical items, use -vv to show\n168\tE       Differing items:\n169\tE       {'value': 42} != {'value': 100}\n170\tE       - 100\n171\tE       ?  -\n172\tE       + 42\n173\t```\n174\t\n175\t### List Index Comparison\n176\t\n177\t```python\n178\tdef test_list_comparison():\n179\t    assert [1, 2, 0, 4] == [1, 2, 3, 4]\n180\t```\n181\t\n182\tOutput:\n183\t```\n184\tFAILED test_example.py:46: in test_list_comparison\n185\t    assert [1, 2, 0, 4] == [1, 2, 3, 4]\n186\tE     AssertionError: assert [1, 2, 0, 4] == [1, 2, 3, 4]\n187\tE       At index 2 diff: 0 != 3\n188\tE       - [1, 2, 3, 4]\n189\tE       ?        ---\n190\tE       + [1, 2, 0, 4]\n191\t```\n192\t\n193\t## Quick Reference\n194\t\n195\t| Use Case | Command |\n196\t|----------|---------|\n197\t| Single failing test | `pytest test_file.py::test_name -vv --tb=short` |\n198\t| All tests in file | `pytest test_file.py -vv --tb=short` |\n199\t| Failed tests only | `pytest test_file.py -vv --tb=short --lf` |\n200\t| With local variables | `pytest test_file.py -vv --tb=short -l` |\n201\t| First failure then stop | `pytest test_file.py -vv --tb=short -x` |\n202\t| Parallel execution (pytest-xdist) | `pytest -n auto -vv --tb=short` |\n203\t\n204\t## Related Documentation\n205\t\n206\t- [bf-1wxppv: Pytest Flags Test Results](../notes/bf-1wxppv-pytest-flags-test-results.md)\n207\t- [bf-1rgu22: Pytest Assertion Output Flags Research](../notes/bf-1rgu22.md)\n208\t- [bf-1wim6c: Pytest Assertion Flags Pytest Command Pattern for Detailed Assertion Output\n\n## Overview\n\nThis document provides the definitive pytest command pattern for producing detailed assertion output with line numbers, expected values, and actual values.\n\n## Optimal Command Pattern\n\n### For Single Tests\n\n```bash\npytest <test_file>::<test_name> -vv --tb=short\n```\n\n### For Entire Test Files\n\n```bash\npytest <test_file> -vv --tb=short\n```\n\n### For Running Only Failed Tests from Last Run\n\n```bash\npytest <test_file> -vv --tb=short --lf\n```\n\n### For All Tests with Maximum Detail\n\n```bash\npytest -vv --tb=short\n```\n\n## Flag Breakdown\n\n| Flag | Purpose | Effect |\n|------|---------|--------|\n| `-vv` | Double verbose | Provides maximum detail in assertion output including character-level diffs |\n| `--tb=short` | Short traceback format | Shows line numbers, assertion details, and diffs in a readable format |\n| `--lf` | Last failed | Rerun only the tests that failed in the last run |\n\n## Why This Combination Works\n\nThe `-vv --tb=short` combination provides:\n\n1. **Exact Line Numbers**: Shows precise file and line location of assertion failures\n   ```\n   test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:22: in test_simple_equality\n   ```\n\n2. **Expected vs Actual Values**: Clear display of what was compared\n   ```\n   assert 'hello there' == 'hello world'\n   ```\n\n3. **Detailed Visual Diffs**: Character-by-character precision with indicators\n   ```\n   - hello world\n   + hello there\n       ^^^^   ^^^^\n   ```\n\n4. **Index Information**: For lists, shows which index differs\n   ```\n   At index 2 diff: 0 != 3\n   ```\n\n5. **Structured Diffs**: Hierarchical view for nested data structures\n   ```\n   user.address.city: Expected 'New York' but got 'San Francisco'\n   ```\n\n## Environment-Specific Considerations\n\n### NixOS (ARMOR Project)\n\n```bash\nnix-shell -p python312Packages.pytest --run \"pytest <test_file>::<test_name> -vv --tb=short\"\n```\n\n### Standard Python Environments\n\n```bash\npytest <test_file>::<test_name> -vv --tb=short\n```\n\n### With Virtual Environments\n\n```bash\nsource venv/bin/activate\npytest <test_file>::<test_name> -vv --tb=short\n```\n\n### With pyproject.toml Configuration\n\nIf you have pytest configured in `pyproject.toml`:\n\n```toml\n[tool.pytest.ini_options]\naddopts = \"-vv --tb=short\"\n```\n\nThen you can run:\n```bash\npytest <test_file>::<test_name>\n```\n\n## Alternative Flag Combinations\n\n### Maximum Detail (with local variables)\n\n```bash\npytest -vv --tb=short -l\n```\n\nThe `-l` flag shows local variables in tracebacks for additional debugging context.\n\n### Long Tracebacks (full stack)\n\n```bash\npytest -vv --tb=long\n```\n\nUse this when you need the full stack trace, not just the failure location.\n\n### Machine-Readable Output\n\n```bash\npytest -vv --tb=short --junit-xml=results.xml\n```\n\nGenerates XML output for CI/CD integration.\n\n## Example Output\n\nHere's what typical output looks like with `-vv --tb=short`:\n\n### Simple Equality\n\n```python\ndef test_simple_equality():\n    assert 'hello there' == 'hello world'\n```\n\nOutput:\n```\nFAILED test_example.py:22: in test_simple_equality\n    assert 'hello there' == 'hello world'\nE     AssertionError: assert 'hello there' == 'hello world'\nE       - hello world\nE       ?    ---  ^^^\nE       + hello there\nE       ?        +++ ^^^^\n```\n\n### Dictionary Comparison\n\n```python\ndef test_dict_equality():\n    assert {'name': 'test', 'value': 42} == {'name': 'test', 'value': 100}\n```\n\nOutput:\n```\nFAILED test_example.py:39: in test_dict_equality\n    assert {'name': 'test', 'value': 42} == {'name': 'test', 'value': 100}\nE     AssertionError: assert {'name': 'test', 'value': 42} == {'name': 'test', 'value': 100}\nE       Omitting 1 identical items, use -vv to show\nE       Differing items:\nE       {'value': 42} != {'value': 100}\nE       - 100\nE       ?  -\nE       + 42\n```\n\n### List Index Comparison\n\n```python\ndef test_list_comparison():\n    assert [1, 2, 0, 4] == [1, 2, 3, 4]\n```\n\nOutput:\n```\nFAILED test_example.py:46: in test_list_comparison\n    assert [1, 2, 0, 4] == [1, 2, 3, 4]\nE     AssertionError: assert [1, 2, 0, 4] == [1, 2, 3, 4]\nE       At index 2 diff: 0 != 3\nE       - [1, 2, 3, 4]\nE       ?        ---\nE       + [1, 2, 0, 4]\n```\n\n## Quick Reference\n\n| Use Case | Command |\n|----------|---------|\n| Single failing test | `pytest test_file.py::test_name -vv --tb=short` |\n| All tests in file | `pytest test_file.py -vv --tb=short` |\n| Failed tests only | `pytest test_file.py -vv --tb=short --lf` |\n| With local variables | `pytest test_file.py -vv --tb=short -l` |\n| First failure then stop | `pytest test_file.py -vv --tb=short -x` |\n| Parallel execution (pytest-xdist) | `pytest -n auto -vv --tb=short` |\n\n## Related Documentation\n\n- [bf-1wxppv: Pytest Flags Test Results](../notes/bf-1wxppv-pytest-flags-test-results.md)\n- [bf-1rgu22: Pytest Assertion Output Flags Research](../notes/bf-1rgu22.md)\n- [bf-1wim6c: Pytest Assertion Flags Research](../notes/bf-1wim6c.md)\n- [Pytest Official Documentation](https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/how-to/output.html)\n\n## Version Information\n\n- **Document Created**: 2026-07-13\n- **Pytest Version Tested**: 8.3.3\n- **Python Version**: 3.12.8\n- **Bead**: bf-33m911\n\n---\n\n**Note**: This pattern has been validated across multiple test scenarios including simple equality, dictionary comparison, list comparison, multiline strings, and nested structures.\n","numLines":221,"startLine":1,"totalLines":221}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_66096a22a713472db35571c6","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Pytest Output Parsing Patterns\n2\t\n3\tThis document defines the regex patterns and structural markers for parsing pytest failure output across different format variations. Designed for ARMOR's test failure analysis system.\n4\t\n5\t## Overview\n6\t\n7\tPytest output has three main parseable sections:\n8\t1. **Test execution section** - Test names and pass/fail status\n9\t2. **Failure details section** - Assertion details with diffs\n10\t3. **Summary section** - Short test summary info\n11\t\n12\t## Section 1: Test Execution Parsing\n13\t\n14\t### Format Variations\n15\t\n16\t#### Verbose format (`-v`, `-vv`)\n17\t```\n18\ttools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py::test_simple_equality FAILED          [  6%]\n19\ttools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py::test_dict_equality FAILED            [ 13%]\n20\t```\n21\t\n22\t#### Concise format (no `-v`)\n23\t```\n24\ttools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py FFFFFFFFFFFFFFF                       [100%]\n25\t```\n26\t\n27\t### Regex Pattern\n28\t\n29\t```python\n30\t# Verbose format: file::test_name STATUS\n31\tVERBOSE_TEST_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?)::(\\w+)\\s+(FAILED|PASSED|ERROR|SKIPPED)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]'\n32\t\n33\t# Concise format: file F/P/E/S marks\n34\tCONCISE_TEST_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?)\\s+([FPE\\.]+)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]'\n35\t```\n36\t\n37\t### Capture Groups\n38\t\n39\t| Group | Content | Example |\n40\t|-------|---------|---------|\n41\t| 1 | File path | `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py` |\n42\t| 2 | Test name | `test_simple_equality` |\n43\t| 3 | Status | `FAILED`, `PASSED`, etc. |\n44\t\n45\t## Section 2: Failure Location Parsing\n46\t\n47\t### Format Variations\n48\t\n49\t#### `--tb=short` (Recommended)\n50\t```\n51\ttools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: in test_simple_equality\n52\t    assert greeting == \"world\", f\"Expected 'world', got '{greeting}'\"\n53\t```\n54\t\n55\t#### `--tb=line` (Best for parsing)\n56\t```\n57\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\n58\t```\n59\t\n60\t#### `--tb=long`\n61\t```\n62\ttools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError\n63\t```\n64\t\n65\t### Regex Patterns\n66\t\n67\t```python\n68\t# Short format: file:line: in test_name\n69\tSHORT_FAILURE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+in\\s+(\\w+)'\n70\t\n71\t# Line format: file:line: AssertionError: message\n72\tLINE_FAILURE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'\n73\t\n74\t# Generic file location (any format)\n75\tFILE_LOCATION_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):'\n76\t```\n77\t\n78\t### Capture Groups\n79\t\n80\t| Group | Content | Example |\n81\t|-------|---------|---------|\n82\t| 1 | File path | `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py` |\n83\t| 2 | Line number | `17` |\n84\t| 3 | Test name or message | `test_simple_equality` or `Expected 'world', got 'hello'` |\n85\t\n86\t## Section 3: Assertion Type Detection\n87\t\n88\t### Common Assertion Patterns\n89\t\n90\t```python\n91\t# Equality assertion (handles both 'E   assert' and plain 'assert' formats)\n92\tASSERT_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+)'\n93\t\n94\t# Pattern: assert X == Y (handles both 'E   assert' and plain 'assert' formats)\n95\tEQUALITY_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+==\\s+(.+)'\n96\t\n97\t# Pattern: assert X in Y (handles both 'E   assert' and plain 'assert' formats)\n98\tCONTAINS_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+in\\s+(.+)'\n99\t\n100\t# Pattern: assert isinstance(X, Y) (handles both 'E   assert' and plain 'assert' formats)\n101\tTYPE_CHECK_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+isinstance\\((.+?),\\s*(.+?)\\)'\n102\t```\n103\t\n104\t### Assertion Type Classification\n105\t\n106\t| Pattern | Type | Example |\n107\t|---------|------|---------|\n108\t| `assert X == Y` | Equality comparison | `assert greeting == \"world\"` |\n109\t| `assert X in Y` | Membership test | `assert \"orange\" in items` |\n110\t| `assert isinstance(X, Y)` | Type check | `assert isinstance(value, int)` |\n111\t| `assert X and Y` | Boolean logic | `assert x and y` |\n112\t| `assert False` | Complex expression | `assert False` (with `where` clause) |\n113\t\n114\t## Section 4: Diff Pattern Parsing\n115\t\n116\t### Expected/Actual Markers\n117\t\n118\tPytest uses `-` for expected values and `+` for actual values.\n119\t\n120\t```python\n121\t# Diff line markers\n122\tDIFF_MINUS_PATTERN = r'^\\s*-\\s*(.+)'\n123\tDIFF_PLUS_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\+\\s*(.+)'\n124\t\n125\t# Character position markers (for multiline strings)\n126\tDIFF_POSITION_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\?\\s*(.+)'\n127\t```\n128\t\n129\t### Index-based Diffs (Lists, Tuples)\n130\t\n131\t```python\n132\t# Pattern: At index N diff: X != Y\n133\tINDEX_DIFF_PATTERN = r'^\\s*At\\s+index\\s+(\\d+)\\s+diff:\\s+(.+?)\\s+!=\\s+(.+)'\n134\t```\n135\t\n136\t### Dictionary Diffs\n137\t\n138\t```python\n139\t# Pattern: Differing items\n140\tDICT_DIFF_HEADER = r'^\\s*Differing items:'\n141\tDICT_DIFF_LINE = r'^\\s*\\{(.+?)\\}\\s+!=\\s+\\{(.+?)\\}'\n142\t```\n143\t\n144\t### Set Diffs\n145\t\n146\t```python\n147\t# Pattern: Extra items in left/right set\n148\tSET_DIFF_LEFT = r'^\\s*Extra items in the left set:'\n149\tSET_DIFF_RIGHT = r'^\\s*Extra items in the right set:'\n150\t```\n151\t\n152\t### Range Diffs\n153\t\n154\t```python\n155\t# Pattern: Right contains one more item: X (singular/plural variations)\n156\t# Handles both \"E     Right contains...\" format and summary format\n157\t# Note: Must match \"one more item\" NOT \"one more more\"\n158\tRANGE_DIFF_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(?:E\\s+)?(Right|Left) contains (?:one more item|\\d+ more items?)\\s*:\\s*(.+)'\n159\t```\n160\t\n161\t### Where Clauses (Type Checks)\n162\t\n163\t```python\n164\t# Pattern: + where False = isinstance(...)\n165\tWHERE_CLAUSE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\+\\s+where\\s+(.+?)\\s+=\\s+(.+)'\n166\t```\n167\t\n168\t## Section 5: Summary Section Parsing\n169\t\n170\t### Short Test Summary Format\n171\t\n172\t```\n173\tFAILED tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py::test_simple_equality - AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\n174\t```\n175\t\n176\t```python\n177\tSUMMARY_PATTERN = r'^(FAILED|PASSED|ERROR|SKIPPED)\\s+(.+?)::(\\w+)\\s+-\\s*(.+)'\n178\t```\n179\t\n180\t### Final Count Line\n181\t\n182\t```\n183\t============================== 15 failed in 0.05s ==============================\n184\t```\n185\t\n186\t```python\n187\tCOUNT_PATTERN = r'=\\s+(\\d+)\\s+(failed|passed|error|skipped)\\s+in\\s+([\\d.]+)s\\s+='\n188\t```\n189\t\n190\t## Section 6: Recommended Parsing Strategy\n191\t\n192\t### For Quick Status Checks\n193\t\n194\tUse `--tb=no` format:\n195\t```bash\n196\tpytest --tb=no test_file.py\n197\t```\n198\t\n199\tParse only the summary line with `COUNT_PATTERN`.\n200\t\n201\t### For Detailed Failure Location\n202\t\n203\tUse `--tb=line` format (most parseable):\n204\t```bash\n205\tpytest --tb=line test_file.py\n206\t```\n207\t\n208\tParse with `LINE_FAILURE_PATTERN` for exact file:line:error message.\n209\t\n210\t### For Full Assertion Details\n211\t\n212\tUse `-vv --tb=short` format:\n213\t```bash\n214\tpytest -vv --tb=short test_file.py\n215\t```\n216\t\n217\tParse in sequence:\n218\t1. `SHORT_FAILURE_PATTERN` for file:line:test_name\n219\t2. `ASSERT_PATTERN` for assertion expression\n220\t3. `EQUALITY_PATTERN`, `CONTAINS_PATTERN`, etc. for assertion type\n221\t4. Diff patterns (`DIFF_MINUS_PATTERN`, `DIFF_PLUS_PATTERN`) for expected/actual values\n222\t\n223\t### For Machine-Readable Output\n224\t\n225\tConsider pytest-json-report plugin (if available):\n226\t```bash\n227\tpytest --json-report --json-report-file=report.json test_file.py\n228\t```\n229\t\n230\t## Section 7: Example Parser Implementation\n231\t\n232\t```python\n233\timport re\n234\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n235\tfrom typing import Optional, List\n236\t\n237\t@dataclass\n238\tclass TestFailure:\n239\t    file: str\n240\t    line: int\n241\t    test_name: Optional[str]\n242\t    error_message: str\n243\t    assertion_type: Optional[str]\n244\t    expected: Optional[str]\n245\t    actual: Optional[str]\n246\t    \n247\t    @classmethod\n248\t    def from_line_format(cls, line: str) -> Optional['TestFailure']:\n249\t        \"\"\"Parse from --tb=line format.\"\"\"\n250\t        match = re.match(LINE_FAILURE_PATTERN, line)\n251\t        if not match:\n252\t            return None\n253\t        \n254\t        file, line_num, message = match.groups()\n255\t        return cls(\n256\t            file=file,\n257\t            line=int(line_num),\n258\t            test_name=None,\n259\t            error_message=message,\n260\t            assertion_type=None,\n261\t            expected=None,\n262\t            actual=None\n263\t        )\n264\t    \n265\t    @classmethod\n266\t    def from_short_format(cls, lines: List[str]) -> Optional['TestFailure']:\n267\t        \"\"\"Parse from --tb=short format.\"\"\"\n268\t        location = None\n269\t        assertion = None\n270\t        expected = None\n271\t        actual = None\n272\t        \n273\t        for line in lines:\n274\t            # Parse location line\n275\t            loc_match = re.match(SHORT_FAILURE_PATTERN, line)\n276\t            if loc_match:\n277\t                file, line_num, test_name = loc_match.groups()\n278\t                location = (file, int(line_num), test_name)\n279\t                continue\n280\t            \n281\t            # Parse assertion\n282\t            assert_match = re.match(ASSERT_PATTERN, line)\n283\t            if assert_match:\n284\t                assertion = assert_match.group(1)\n285\t                continue\n286\t            \n287\t            # Parse expected\n288\t            minus_match = re.match(DIFF_MINUS_PATTERN, line)\n289\t            if minus_match and not expected:\n290\t                expected = minus_match.group(1)\n291\t                continue\n292\t            \n293\t            # Parse actual\n294\t            plus_match = re.match(DIFF_PLUS_PATTERN, line)\n295\t            if plus_match and not actual:\n296\t                actual = plus_match.group(1)\n297\t        \n298\t        if not location:\n299\t            return None\n300\t        \n301\t        return cls(\n302\t            file=location[0],\n303\t            line=location[1],\n304\t            test_name=location[2],\n305\t            error_message=assertion or \"\",\n306\t            assertion_type=cls._classify_assertion(assertion),\n307\t            expected=expected,\n308\t            actual=actual\n309\t        )\n310\t    \n311\t    @staticmethod\n312\t    def _classify_assertion(assertion: Optional[str]) -> Optional[str]:\n313\t        \"\"\"Classify assertion type.\"\"\"\n314\t        if not assertion:\n315\t            return None\n316\t        \n317\t        if ' == ' in assertion:\n318\t            return 'equality'\n319\t        elif ' in ' in assertion:\n320\t            return 'contains'\n321\t        elif 'isinstance' in assertion:\n322\t            return 'type_check'\n323\t        elif ' and ' in assertion or ' or ' in assertion:\n324\t            return 'boolean'\n325\t        else:\n326\t            return 'unknown'\n327\t```\n328\t\n329\t## Section 8: Edge Cases and Gotchas\n330\t\n331\t### Truncated Output\n332\t\n333\tWhen using `-v` without `-vv`, pytest truncates diffs:\n334\t```\n335\t...Full output truncated (14 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show\n336\t```\n337\t\n338\t**Pattern:** `r'^\\s+\\.\\.\\.Full output truncated\\s+\\((\\d+) lines hidden\\)'`\n339\t\n340\t### Ellipsis in Output\n341\t\n342\tPytest uses `...` to indicate truncated sections:\n343\t```\n344\tassert {'age': 25, '...name': 'Bob'} == {'age': 30, '...ame': 'Alice'}\n345\t```\n346\t\n347\t**Pattern:** `r'\\.\\.\\.'` (three consecutive dots in values)\n348\t\n349\t### Multiline String Escapes\n350\t\n351\tMultiline strings may show escaped characters:\n352\t```\n353\tassert '\\n    This i...ontent.\\n    ' == '\\n    This i... lines.\\n    '\n354\t```\n355\t\n356\t**Pattern:** Look for `\\n` and other escape sequences.\n357\t\n358\t### Platform-Specific Paths\n359\t\n360\tPaths may use different separators on different platforms:\n361\t- Unix: `/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_file.py`\n362\t- Windows: `C:\\Users\\coding\\ARMOR\\tools\\test_file.py`\n363\t\n364\t**Solution:** Use `os.path.normpath()` for consistent path handling.\n365\t\n366\t## Section 9: Regex Pattern Reference\n367\t\n368\t### Core Patterns\n369\t\n370\t```python\n371\t# File location (any format)\n372\tFILE_LOCATION_PATTERN = r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'\n373\t\n374\t# Test execution (verbose)\n375\tVERBOSE_TEST_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?)::(\\w+)\\s+(FAILED|PASSED|ERROR|SKIPPED)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]'\n376\t\n377\t# Test execution (concise)\n378\tCONCISE_TEST_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?)\\s+([FPE\\.]+)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]'\n379\t\n380\t# Failure (short format)\n381\tSHORT_FAILURE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+in\\s+(\\w+)'\n382\t\n383\t# Failure (line format)\n384\tLINE_FAILURE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'\n385\t\n386\t# Summary\n387\tSUMMARY_PATTERN = r'^(FAILED|PASSED|ERROR|SKIPPED)\\s+(.+?)::(\\w+)\\s+-\\s*(.+)'\n388\t\n389\t# Count\n390\tCOUNT_PATTERN = r'=\\s+(\\d+)\\s+(failed|passed|error|skipped)\\s+in\\s+([\\d.]+)s\\s+='\n391\t```\n392\t\n393\t### Assertion Patterns\n394\t\n395\t```python\n396\tASSERT_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+)'\n397\tEQUALITY_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+==\\s+(.+)'\n398\tCONTAINS_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+in\\s+(.+)'\n399\tTYPE_CHECK_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+isinstance\\((.+?),\\s*(.+?)\\)'\n400\t```\n401\t\n402\t### Diff Patterns\n403\t\n404\t```python\n405\tDIFF_MINUS_PATTERN = r'^\\s*-\\s*(.+)'\n406\tDIFF_PLUS_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\+\\s*(.+)'\n407\tDIFF_POSITION_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\?\\s*(.+)'\n408\tINDEX_DIFF_PATTERN = r'^\\s*At\\s+index\\s+(\\d+)\\s+diff:\\s+(.+?)\\s+!=\\s+(.+)'\n409\tDICT_DIFF_HEADER = r'^\\s*Differing items:'\n410\tSET_DIFF_LEFT = r'^\\s*Extra items in the left set:'\n411\tSET_DIFF_RIGHT = r'^\\s*Extra items in the right set:'\n412\tRANGE_DIFF_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(?:E\\s+)?(Right|Left) contains (?:one more item|\\d+ more items?)\\s*:\\s*(.+)'\n413\tWHERE_CLAUSE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\+\\s+where\\s+(.+?)\\s+=\\s+(.+)'\n414\t```\n415\t\n416\t### Section Markers\n417\t\n418\t```python\n419\tFAILURES_SECTION = r'^===+ FAILURES ===+'\n420\tSUMMARY_SECTION = r'^===+ short test summary info ===+'\n421\tSESSION_START = r'^===+ test session starts ===+'\n422\tSESSION_END = r'^===+ \\d+ (?:failed|passed) in [\\d.]+s ===+'\n423\t```\n424\t\n425\t## Appendix: Quick Reference\n426\t\n427\t### Parse Priority by Format\n428\t\n429\t1. **Easiest** - `--tb=line`: One line per failure with full error message\n430\t2. **Easy** - `--tb=no`: Just summary, use for count only\n431\t3. **Medium** - `-vv --tb=short`: Full details but multi-line per failure\n432\t4. **Hard** - `--tb=long`: Variable stack depth, complex parsing\n433\t5. **Variable** - `--tb=auto`: Inconsistent between failures\n434\t\n435\t### Recommended Format for ARMOR\n436\t\n437\tFor ARMOR's test failure analysis, use:\n438\t```bash\n439\tpytest -vv --tb=short --tb=line test_file.py\n440\t```\n441\t\n442\tThis provides:\n443\t- Detailed assertion rewriting for value extraction (`-vv`)\n444\t- Parseable failure locations (`--tb=line`)\n445\t- Complete diffs without truncation\n446\t\n447\t### Test Coverage\n448\t\n449\tAll patterns tested against 15 assertion types:\n450\t1. ✅ Simple equality\n451\t2. ✅ Dictionary equality  \n452\t3. ✅ List comparison\n453\t4. ✅ Multiline strings\n454\t5. ✅ Numeric comparison\n455\t6. ✅ Membership testing\n456\t7. ✅ Long sequences\n457\t8. ✅ Nested structures\n458\t9. ✅ Floating-point comparison\n459\t10. ✅ Boolean logic\n460\t11. ✅ String operations\n461\t12. ✅ Type checking\n462\t13. ✅ Set (Pytest Failure Output Samples)\n470\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"50e58331-b33d-40bc-9690-f4679b077403","uuid":"e75e1a96-bc22-49a4-ad12-44b176483fed","timestamp":"2026-07-14T01:50:43.032Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/pytest_parser.md","content":"# Pytest Output Parsing Patterns\n\nThis document defines the regex patterns and structural markers for parsing pytest failure output across different format variations. Designed for ARMOR's test failure analysis system.\n\n## Overview\n\nPytest output has three main parseable sections:\n1. **Test execution section** - Test names and pass/fail status\n2. **Failure details section** - Assertion details with diffs\n3. **Summary section** - Short test summary info\n\n## Section 1: Test Execution Parsing\n\n### Format Variations\n\n#### Verbose format (`-v`, `-vv`)\n```\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py::test_simple_equality FAILED          [  6%]\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py::test_dict_equality FAILED            [ 13%]\n```\n\n#### Concise format (no `-v`)\n```\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py FFFFFFFFFFFFFFF                       [100%]\n```\n\n### Regex Pattern\n\n```python\n# Verbose format: file::test_name STATUS\nVERBOSE_TEST_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?)::(\\w+)\\s+(FAILED|PASSED|ERROR|SKIPPED)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]'\n\n# Concise format: file F/P/E/S marks\nCONCISE_TEST_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?)\\s+([FPE\\.]+)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]'\n```\n\n### Capture Groups\n\n| Group | Content | Example |\n|-------|---------|---------|\n| 1 | File path | `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py` |\n| 2 | Test name | `test_simple_equality` |\n| 3 | Status | `FAILED`, `PASSED`, etc. |\n\n## Section 2: Failure Location Parsing\n\n### Format Variations\n\n#### `--tb=short` (Recommended)\n```\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: in test_simple_equality\n    assert greeting == \"world\", f\"Expected 'world', got '{greeting}'\"\n```\n\n#### `--tb=line` (Best for parsing)\n```\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\n```\n\n#### `--tb=long`\n```\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError\n```\n\n### Regex Patterns\n\n```python\n# Short format: file:line: in test_name\nSHORT_FAILURE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+in\\s+(\\w+)'\n\n# Line format: file:line: AssertionError: message\nLINE_FAILURE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'\n\n# Generic file location (any format)\nFILE_LOCATION_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):'\n```\n\n### Capture Groups\n\n| Group | Content | Example |\n|-------|---------|---------|\n| 1 | File path | `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py` |\n| 2 | Line number | `17` |\n| 3 | Test name or message | `test_simple_equality` or `Expected 'world', got 'hello'` |\n\n## Section 3: Assertion Type Detection\n\n### Common Assertion Patterns\n\n```python\n# Equality assertion (handles both 'E   assert' and plain 'assert' formats)\nASSERT_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+)'\n\n# Pattern: assert X == Y (handles both 'E   assert' and plain 'assert' formats)\nEQUALITY_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+==\\s+(.+)'\n\n# Pattern: assert X in Y (handles both 'E   assert' and plain 'assert' formats)\nCONTAINS_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+in\\s+(.+)'\n\n# Pattern: assert isinstance(X, Y) (handles both 'E   assert' and plain 'assert' formats)\nTYPE_CHECK_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+isinstance\\((.+?),\\s*(.+?)\\)'\n```\n\n### Assertion Type Classification\n\n| Pattern | Type | Example |\n|---------|------|---------|\n| `assert X == Y` | Equality comparison | `assert greeting == \"world\"` |\n| `assert X in Y` | Membership test | `assert \"orange\" in items` |\n| `assert isinstance(X, Y)` | Type check | `assert isinstance(value, int)` |\n| `assert X and Y` | Boolean logic | `assert x and y` |\n| `assert False` | Complex expression | `assert False` (with `where` clause) |\n\n## Section 4: Diff Pattern Parsing\n\n### Expected/Actual Markers\n\nPytest uses `-` for expected values and `+` for actual values.\n\n```python\n# Diff line markers\nDIFF_MINUS_PATTERN = r'^\\s*-\\s*(.+)'\nDIFF_PLUS_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\+\\s*(.+)'\n\n# Character position markers (for multiline strings)\nDIFF_POSITION_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\?\\s*(.+)'\n```\n\n### Index-based Diffs (Lists, Tuples)\n\n```python\n# Pattern: At index N diff: X != Y\nINDEX_DIFF_PATTERN = r'^\\s*At\\s+index\\s+(\\d+)\\s+diff:\\s+(.+?)\\s+!=\\s+(.+)'\n```\n\n### Dictionary Diffs\n\n```python\n# Pattern: Differing items\nDICT_DIFF_HEADER = r'^\\s*Differing items:'\nDICT_DIFF_LINE = r'^\\s*\\{(.+?)\\}\\s+!=\\s+\\{(.+?)\\}'\n```\n\n### Set Diffs\n\n```python\n# Pattern: Extra items in left/right set\nSET_DIFF_LEFT = r'^\\s*Extra items in the left set:'\nSET_DIFF_RIGHT = r'^\\s*Extra items in the right set:'\n```\n\n### Range Diffs\n\n```python\n# Pattern: Right contains one more item: X (singular/plural variations)\n# Handles both \"E     Right contains...\" format and summary format\n# Note: Must match \"one more item\" NOT \"one more more\"\nRANGE_DIFF_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(?:E\\s+)?(Right|Left) contains (?:one more item|\\d+ more items?)\\s*:\\s*(.+)'\n```\n\n### Where Clauses (Type Checks)\n\n```python\n# Pattern: + where False = isinstance(...)\nWHERE_CLAUSE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\+\\s+where\\s+(.+?)\\s+=\\s+(.+)'\n```\n\n## Section 5: Summary Section Parsing\n\n### Short Test Summary Format\n\n```\nFAILED tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py::test_simple_equality - AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\n```\n\n```python\nSUMMARY_PATTERN = r'^(FAILED|PASSED|ERROR|SKIPPED)\\s+(.+?)::(\\w+)\\s+-\\s*(.+)'\n```\n\n### Final Count Line\n\n```\n============================== 15 failed in 0.05s ==============================\n```\n\n```python\nCOUNT_PATTERN = r'=\\s+(\\d+)\\s+(failed|passed|error|skipped)\\s+in\\s+([\\d.]+)s\\s+='\n```\n\n## Section 6: Recommended Parsing Strategy\n\n### For Quick Status Checks\n\nUse `--tb=no` format:\n```bash\npytest --tb=no test_file.py\n```\n\nParse only the summary line with `COUNT_PATTERN`.\n\n### For Detailed Failure Location\n\nUse `--tb=line` format (most parseable):\n```bash\npytest --tb=line test_file.py\n```\n\nParse with `LINE_FAILURE_PATTERN` for exact file:line:error message.\n\n### For Full Assertion Details\n\nUse `-vv --tb=short` format:\n```bash\npytest -vv --tb=short test_file.py\n```\n\nParse in sequence:\n1. `SHORT_FAILURE_PATTERN` for file:line:test_name\n2. `ASSERT_PATTERN` for assertion expression\n3. `EQUALITY_PATTERN`, `CONTAINS_PATTERN`, etc. for assertion type\n4. Diff patterns (`DIFF_MINUS_PATTERN`, `DIFF_PLUS_PATTERN`) for expected/actual values\n\n### For Machine-Readable Output\n\nConsider pytest-json-report plugin (if available):\n```bash\npytest --json-report --json-report-file=report.json test_file.py\n```\n\n## Section 7: Example Parser Implementation\n\n```python\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom typing import Optional, List\n\n@dataclass\nclass TestFailure:\n    file: str\n    line: int\n    test_name: Optional[str]\n    error_message: str\n    assertion_type: Optional[str]\n    expected: Optional[str]\n    actual: Optional[str]\n    \n    @classmethod\n    def from_line_format(cls, line: str) -> Optional['TestFailure']:\n        \"\"\"Parse from --tb=line format.\"\"\"\n        match = re.match(LINE_FAILURE_PATTERN, line)\n        if not match:\n            return None\n        \n        file, line_num, message = match.groups()\n        return cls(\n            file=file,\n            line=int(line_num),\n            test_name=None,\n            error_message=message,\n            assertion_type=None,\n            expected=None,\n            actual=None\n        )\n    \n    @classmethod\n    def from_short_format(cls, lines: List[str]) -> Optional['TestFailure']:\n        \"\"\"Parse from --tb=short format.\"\"\"\n        location = None\n        assertion = None\n        expected = None\n        actual = None\n        \n        for line in lines:\n            # Parse location line\n            loc_match = re.match(SHORT_FAILURE_PATTERN, line)\n            if loc_match:\n                file, line_num, test_name = loc_match.groups()\n                location = (file, int(line_num), test_name)\n                continue\n            \n            # Parse assertion\n            assert_match = re.match(ASSERT_PATTERN, line)\n            if assert_match:\n                assertion = assert_match.group(1)\n                continue\n            \n            # Parse expected\n            minus_match = re.match(DIFF_MINUS_PATTERN, line)\n            if minus_match and not expected:\n                expected = minus_match.group(1)\n                continue\n            \n            # Parse actual\n            plus_match = re.match(DIFF_PLUS_PATTERN, line)\n            if plus_match and not actual:\n                actual = plus_match.group(1)\n        \n        if not location:\n            return None\n        \n        return cls(\n            file=location[0],\n            line=location[1],\n            test_name=location[2],\n            error_message=assertion or \"\",\n            assertion_type=cls._classify_assertion(assertion),\n            expected=expected,\n            actual=actual\n        )\n    \n    @staticmethod\n    def _classify_assertion(assertion: Optional[str]) -> Optional[str]:\n        \"\"\"Classify assertion type.\"\"\"\n        if not assertion:\n            return None\n        \n        if ' == ' in assertion:\n            return 'equality'\n        elif ' in ' in assertion:\n            return 'contains'\n        elif 'isinstance' in assertion:\n            return 'type_check'\n        elif ' and ' in assertion or ' or ' in assertion:\n            return 'boolean'\n        else:\n            return 'unknown'\n```\n\n## Section 8: Edge Cases and Gotchas\n\n### Truncated Output\n\nWhen using `-v` without `-vv`, pytest truncates diffs:\n```\n...Full output truncated (14 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show\n```\n\n**Pattern:** `r'^\\s+\\.\\.\\.Full output truncated\\s+\\((\\d+) lines hidden\\)'`\n\n### Ellipsis in Output\n\nPytest uses `...` to indicate truncated sections:\n```\nassert {'age': 25, '...name': 'Bob'} == {'age': 30, '...ame': 'Alice'}\n```\n\n**Pattern:** `r'\\.\\.\\.'` (three consecutive dots in values)\n\n### Multiline String Escapes\n\nMultiline strings may show escaped characters:\n```\nassert '\\n    This i...ontent.\\n    ' == '\\n    This i... lines.\\n    '\n```\n\n**Pattern:** Look for `\\n` and other escape sequences.\n\n### Platform-Specific Paths\n\nPaths may use different separators on different platforms:\n- Unix: `/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_file.py`\n- Windows: `C:\\Users\\coding\\ARMOR\\tools\\test_file.py`\n\n**Solution:** Use `os.path.normpath()` for consistent path handling.\n\n## Section 9: Regex Pattern Reference\n\n### Core Patterns\n\n```python\n# File location (any format)\nFILE_LOCATION_PATTERN = r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'\n\n# Test execution (verbose)\nVERBOSE_TEST_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?)::(\\w+)\\s+(FAILED|PASSED|ERROR|SKIPPED)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]'\n\n# Test execution (concise)\nCONCISE_TEST_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?)\\s+([FPE\\.]+)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]'\n\n# Failure (short format)\nSHORT_FAILURE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+in\\s+(\\w+)'\n\n# Failure (line format)\nLINE_FAILURE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'\n\n# Summary\nSUMMARY_PATTERN = r'^(FAILED|PASSED|ERROR|SKIPPED)\\s+(.+?)::(\\w+)\\s+-\\s*(.+)'\n\n# Count\nCOUNT_PATTERN = r'=\\s+(\\d+)\\s+(failed|passed|error|skipped)\\s+in\\s+([\\d.]+)s\\s+='\n```\n\n### Assertion Patterns\n\n```python\nASSERT_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+)'\nEQUALITY_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+==\\s+(.+)'\nCONTAINS_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+in\\s+(.+)'\nTYPE_CHECK_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+isinstance\\((.+?),\\s*(.+?)\\)'\n```\n\n### Diff Patterns\n\n```python\nDIFF_MINUS_PATTERN = r'^\\s*-\\s*(.+)'\nDIFF_PLUS_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\+\\s*(.+)'\nDIFF_POSITION_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\?\\s*(.+)'\nINDEX_DIFF_PATTERN = r'^\\s*At\\s+index\\s+(\\d+)\\s+diff:\\s+(.+?)\\s+!=\\s+(.+)'\nDICT_DIFF_HEADER = r'^\\s*Differing items:'\nSET_DIFF_LEFT = r'^\\s*Extra items in the left set:'\nSET_DIFF_RIGHT = r'^\\s*Extra items in the right set:'\nRANGE_DIFF_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(?:E\\s+)?(Right|Left) contains (?:one more item|\\d+ more items?)\\s*:\\s*(.+)'\nWHERE_CLAUSE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\+\\s+where\\s+(.+?)\\s+=\\s+(.+)'\n```\n\n### Section Markers\n\n```python\nFAILURES_SECTION = r'^===+ FAILURES ===+'\nSUMMARY_SECTION = r'^===+ short test summary info ===+'\nSESSION_START = r'^===+ test session starts ===+'\nSESSION_END = r'^===+ \\d+ (?:failed|passed) in [\\d.]+s ===+'\n```\n\n## Appendix: Quick Reference\n\n### Parse Priority by Format\n\n1. **Easiest** - `--tb=line`: One line per failure with full error message\n2. **Easy** - `--tb=no`: Just summary, use for count only\n3. **Medium** - `-vv --tb=short`: Full details but multi-line per failure\n4. **Hard** - `--tb=long`: Variable stack depth, complex parsing\n5. **Variable** - `--tb=auto`: Inconsistent between failures\n\n### Recommended Format for ARMOR\n\nFor ARMOR's test failure analysis, use:\n```bash\npytest -vv --tb=short --tb=line test_file.py\n```\n\nThis provides:\n- Detailed assertion rewriting for value extraction (`-vv`)\n- Parseable failure locations (`--tb=line`)\n- Complete diffs without truncation\n\n### Test Coverage\n\nAll patterns tested against 15 assertion types:\n1. ✅ Simple equality\n2. ✅ Dictionary equality  \n3. ✅ List comparison\n4. ✅ Multiline strings\n5. ✅ Numeric comparison\n6. ✅ Membership testing\n7. ✅ Long sequences\n8. ✅ Nested structures\n9. ✅ Floating-point comparison\n10. ✅ Boolean logic\n11. ✅ String operations\n12. ✅ Type checking\n13. ✅ Set operations\n14. ✅ Range comparison\n15. ✅ Tuple comparison\n\n---\n\n**Generated:** 2026-07-13 for ARMOR project (bead: bf-63vue2)  \n**Parent Task:** bf-1ym8jw (Pytest Failure Output Designed for ARMOR's test failure analysis system.\n\n## Overview\n\nPytest output has three main parseable sections:\n1. **Test execution section** - Test names and pass/fail status\n2. **Failure details section** - Assertion details with diffs\n3. **Summary section** - Short test summary info\n\n## Section 1: Test Execution Parsing\n\n### Format Variations\n\n#### Verbose format (`-v`, `-vv`)\n```\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py::test_simple_equality FAILED          [  6%]\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py::test_dict_equality FAILED            [ 13%]\n```\n\n#### Concise format (no `-v`)\n```\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py FFFFFFFFFFFFFFF                       [100%]\n```\n\n### Regex Pattern\n\n```python\n# Verbose format: file::test_name STATUS\nVERBOSE_TEST_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?)::(\\w+)\\s+(FAILED|PASSED|ERROR|SKIPPED)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]'\n\n# Concise format: file F/P/E/S marks\nCONCISE_TEST_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?)\\s+([FPE\\.]+)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]'\n```\n\n### Capture Groups\n\n| Group | Content | Example |\n|-------|---------|---------|\n| 1 | File path | `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py` |\n| 2 | Test name | `test_simple_equality` |\n| 3 | Status | `FAILED`, `PASSED`, etc. |\n\n## Section 2: Failure Location Parsing\n\n### Format Variations\n\n#### `--tb=short` (Recommended)\n```\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: in test_simple_equality\n    assert greeting == \"world\", f\"Expected 'world', got '{greeting}'\"\n```\n\n#### `--tb=line` (Best for parsing)\n```\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\n```\n\n#### `--tb=long`\n```\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError\n```\n\n### Regex Patterns\n\n```python\n# Short format: file:line: in test_name\nSHORT_FAILURE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+in\\s+(\\w+)'\n\n# Line format: file:line: AssertionError: message\nLINE_FAILURE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'\n\n# Generic file location (any format)\nFILE_LOCATION_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):'\n```\n\n### Capture Groups\n\n| Group | Content | Example |\n|-------|---------|---------|\n| 1 | File path | `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py` |\n| 2 | Line number | `17` |\n| 3 | Test name or message | `test_simple_equality` or `Expected 'world', got 'hello'` |\n\n## Section 3: Assertion Type Detection\n\n### Common Assertion Patterns\n\n```python\n# Equality assertion (handles both 'E   assert' and plain 'assert' formats)\nASSERT_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+)'\n\n# Pattern: assert X == Y (handles both 'E   assert' and plain 'assert' formats)\nEQUALITY_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+==\\s+(.+)'\n\n# Pattern: assert X in Y (handles both 'E   assert' and plain 'assert' formats)\nCONTAINS_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+in\\s+(.+)'\n\n# Pattern: assert isinstance(X, Y) (handles both 'E   assert' and plain 'assert' Pytest Output Parsing Patterns\n\nThis document defines the regex patterns and structural markers for parsing pytest failure output across different format variations. Designed for ARMOR's test failure analysis system.\n\n## Overview\n\nPytest output has three main parseable sections:\n1. **Test execution section** - Test names and pass/fail status\n2. **Failure details section** - Assertion details with diffs\n3. **Summary section** - Short test summary info\n\n## Section 1: Test Execution Parsing\n\n### Format Variations\n\n#### Verbose format (`-v`, `-vv`)\n```\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py::test_simple_equality FAILED          [  6%]\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py::test_dict_equality FAILED            [ 13%]\n```\n\n#### Concise format (no `-v`)\n```\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py FFFFFFFFFFFFFFF                       [100%]\n```\n\n### Regex Pattern\n\n```python\n# Verbose format: file::test_name STATUS\nVERBOSE_TEST_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?)::(\\w+)\\s+(FAILED|PASSED|ERROR|SKIPPED)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]'\n\n# Concise format: file F/P/E/S marks\nCONCISE_TEST_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?)\\s+([FPE\\.]+)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]'\n```\n\n### Capture Groups\n\n| Group | Content | Example |\n|-------|---------|---------|\n| 1 | File path | `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py` |\n| 2 | Test name | `test_simple_equality` |\n| 3 | Status | `FAILED`, `PASSED`, etc. |\n\n## Section 2: Failure Location Parsing\n\n### Format Variations\n\n#### `--tb=short` (Recommended)\n```\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: in test_simple_equality\n    assert greeting == \"world\", f\"Expected 'world', got '{greeting}'\"\n```\n\n#### `--tb=line` (Best for parsing)\n```\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\n```\n\n#### `--tb=long`\n```\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError\n```\n\n### Regex Patterns\n\n```python\n# Short format: file:line: in test_name\nSHORT_FAILURE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+in\\s+(\\w+)'\n\n# Line format: file:line: AssertionError: message\nLINE_FAILURE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'\n\n# Generic file location (any format)\nFILE_LOCATION_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):'\n```\n\n### Capture Groups\n\n| Group | Content | Example |\n|-------|---------|---------|\n| 1 | File path | `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py` |\n| 2 | Line number | `17` |\n| 3 | Test name or message | `test_simple_equality` or `Expected 'world', got 'hello'` |\n\n## Section 3: Assertion Type Detection\n\n### Common Assertion Patterns\n\n```python\n# Equality assertion (handles both 'E   assert' and plain 'assert' formats)\nASSERT_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+)'\n\n# Pattern: assert X == Y (handles both 'E   assert' and plain 'assert' formats)\nEQUALITY_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+==\\s+(.+)'\n\n# Pattern: assert X in Y (handles both 'E   assert' and plain 'assert' formats)\nCONTAINS_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+in\\s+(.+)'\n\n# Pattern: assert isinstance(X, Y) (handles both 'E   assert' and plain 'assert' formats)","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_20260714095131ac021424165b4f7f","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_aa6cebb78e9e45a6b55eb0c7","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/pytest_patterns.md","content":"# Pytest Output Patterns Specification\n\n**Document Version:** 1.0  \n**Date:** 2026-07-13  \n**Task:** bf-5x5xz1 (Research and document pytest output formats)  \n**Status:** ✅ COMPLETE\n\n## Overview\n\nThis specification documents the exact patterns and variations found in pytest failure output across different traceback formats. It provides sample outputs, regex patterns, and edge cases for reliable machine parsing.\n\n## Scope\n\nThis specification covers pytest version 8.3.3 running on Python 3.12.8 with the following command patterns:\n\n```bash\n# Standard detailed format\npytest -vv --tb=short <test_file>\n\n# One-line format  \npytest --tb=line <test_file>\n\n# Summary-only format\npytest --tb=no <test_file>\n```\n\n## Format 1: Short Traceback (`--tb=short`)\n\n### Command Pattern\n\n```bash\npytest -vv --tb=short test_file.py::test_name\n```\n\n### Sample Output\n\n```\n=================================== FAILURES ===================================\n_____________________________ test_simple_equality _____________________________\n\ntest_pytest_flags_minimal.py:22: in test_simple_equality\n    assert greeting == \"world\"\nE   AssertionError: assert 'hello' == 'world'\nE     - world\nE     + hello\n```\n\n### Core Patterns\n\n#### File Location Pattern\n\n**Regex:**\n```regex\n^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+in\\s+(\\w+)\n```\n\n**Example Match:**\n- Input: `test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:22: in test_simple_equality`\n- Capture Groups:\n  - Group 1: `test_pytest_flags_minimal.py` (file path)\n  - Group 2: `22` (line number)\n  - Group 3: `test_simple_equality` (test name)\n\n**Edge Cases:**\n- Absolute paths: `/home/user/project/tests/test_file.py:15: in test_func`\n- Relative paths: `tests/module/test_file.py:42: in test_nested`\n- Windows paths: `C:\\Users\\project\\tests\\test_file.py:10: in test_windows`\n\n#### Assertion Pattern\n\n**Regex:**\n```regex\n^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+)\n```\n\n**Example Matches:**\n- `    assert greeting == \"world\"` → `greeting == \"world\"`\n- `E   assert 'hello' == 'world'` → `'hello' == 'world'`\n- `assert 5 == 10` → `5 == 10`\n\n**Edge Cases:**\n- Multiline assertions may be truncated with `...`\n- Complex boolean expressions: `assert x > 0 and y < 10`\n\n#### Equality Assertion Pattern\n\n**Regex:**\n```regex\n^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+==\\s+(.+)\n```\n\n**Example Match:**\n- Input: `E   assert 'hello' == 'world'`\n- Capture Groups:\n  - Group 1: `'hello'` (left side/actual)\n  - Group 2: `'world'` (right side/expected)\n\n#### Diff Pattern (Expected/Actual)\n\n**Regex:**\n```regex\n^\\s*-\\s*(.+)   # Expected value (marked with -)\n^\\s*\\+\\s*(.+)  # Actual value (marked with +)\n```\n\n**Example Matches:**\n- `E     - world` → `world` (expected)\n- `E     + hello` → `hello` (actual)\n\n**Edge Cases:**\n- Character position markers: `E     ?    +++++`\n- Multiline strings show escaped newlines: `\\n    content\\n`\n\n### Index Diff Pattern (Lists/Tuples)\n\n**Regex:**\n```regex\n^\\s*At\\s+index\\s+(\\d+)\\s+diff:\\s+(.+?)\\s+!=\\s+(.+)\n```\n\n**Example Match:**\n- Input: `E       At index 2 diff: 0 != 3`\n- Capture Groups:\n  - Group 1: `2` (index position)\n  - Group 2: `0` (actual value)\n  - Group 3: `3` (expected value)\n\n**Edge Cases:**\n- Multiple indices may differ\n- Index shown with context: `At index 5 diff: 10 != 5` (whitespace variations)\n\n### Dictionary Diff Pattern\n\n**Header Regex:**\n```regex\n^\\s*Differing items:\n```\n\n**Item Line Regex:**\n```regex\n^\\s*\\{(.+?)\\}\\s+!=\\s+\\{(.+?)\\}\n```\n\n**Example Match:**\n- Input: `E       {'value': 42} != {'value': 100}`\n- Capture Groups:\n  - Group 1: `'value': 42` (actual)\n  - Group 2: `'value': 100` (expected)\n\n### Range Diff Pattern\n\n**Regex:**\n```regex\n^\\s*(?:E\\s+)?(Right|Left) contains (?:one more item|\\d+ more items?)\\s*:\\s*(.+)\n```\n\n**Example Matches:**\n- Input: `E     Right contains one more item: 5`\n- Input: `Right contains 2 more items: 7, 9`\n- Capture Groups:\n  - Group 1: `Right` or `Left` (which side has more)\n  - Group 2: `5` or `7, 9` (the extra items)\n\n**Important:** Pattern must match \"one more item\" NOT \"one more more\" to avoid false matches.\n\n### Type Check Pattern\n\n**Regex:**\n```regex\n^\\s*\\+\\s+where\\s+(.+?)\\s+=\\s+(.+)\n```\n\n**Example Match:**\n- Input: `E       + where False = isinstance(5, str)`\n- Capture Groups:\n  - Group 1: `False` (condition result)\n  - Group 2: `isinstance(5, str)` (check expression)\n\n### Set Diff Pattern\n\n**Left Set Regex:**\n```regex\n^\\s*Extra items in the left set:\\s*\\{(.+)\\}\n```\n\n**Right Set Regex:**\n```regex\n^\\s*Extra items in the left set:\\s*\\{(.+)\\}\n```\n\n**Example Matches:**\n- Input: `E       Extra items in the left set: {1, 2}`\n- Input: `E       Extra items in the right set: {4, 5}`\n\n## Format 2: Line Traceback (`--tb=line`)\n\n### Command Pattern\n\n```bash\npytest --tb=line test_file.py::test_name\n```\n\n### Sample Output\n\n```\n=================================== FAILURES ===================================\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:39: AssertionError: assert {'name': 'test', 'value': 42} == {'name': 'test', 'value': 100}\n```\n\n### Core Patterns\n\n#### Line Failure Pattern\n\n**Regex:**\n```regex\n^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+AssertionError:\\s*(.+)\n```\n\n**Example Match:**\n- Input: `/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'`\n- Capture Groups:\n  - Group 1: `/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py` (file path)\n  - Group 2: `17` (line number)\n  - Group 3: `Expected 'world', got 'hello'` (error message)\n\n**Edge Cases:**\n- No indentation on error lines (unlike --tb=short)\n- Error message may contain assertion details\n- No diff information available in this format\n\n## Format 3: No Traceback (`--tb=no`)\n\n### Command Pattern\n\n```bash\npytest --tb=no test_file.py\n```\n\n### Sample Output\n\n```\nFAILED test_pytest_flags_minimal.py::test_simple_equality - AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\nFAILED test_pytest_flags_minimal.py::test_dict_equality - AssertionError: assert {'name': 'test', 'value': 42} == {'name': 'test', 'value': 100}\n============================== 2 failed in 0.03s ==============================\n```\n\n### Core Patterns\n\n#### Summary Pattern\n\n**Regex:**\n```regex\n^(FAILED|PASSED|ERROR|SKIPPED)\\s+(.+?)::(\\w+)\\s+-\\s*(.+)\n```\n\n**Example Match:**\n- Input: `FAILED test_pytest_flags_minimal.py::test_simple_equality - AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'`\n- Capture Groups:\n  - Group 1: `FAILED` (status)\n  - Group 2: `test_pytest_flags_minimal.py` (file path)\n  - Group 3: `test_simple_equality` (test name)\n  - Group 4: `AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'` (error message)\n\n#### Count Pattern\n\n**Regex:**\n```regex\n=\\s+(\\d+)\\s+(failed|passed|error|skipped)\\s+in\\s+([\\d.]+)s\\s+=\n```\n\n**Example Match:**\n- Input: `============================== 2 failed in 0.03s ==============================`\n- Capture Groups:\n  - Group 1: `2` (count)\n  - Group 2: `failed` (status)\n  - Group 3: `0.03` (duration in seconds)\n\n## Edge Cases and Format Variations\n\n### 1. Truncated Output\n\nWhen using `-v` without `-vv`, pytest truncates long diffs:\n\n**Pattern:**\n```regex\n^\\s+\\.\\.\\.Full output truncated\\s+\\((\\d+) lines hidden\\),\\s+use '-vv' to show\n```\n\n**Example:**\n```\n...Full output truncated (14 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show\n```\n\n### 2. Ellipsis in Nested Structures\n\nPytest uses `...` to indicate omitted content in complex structures:\n\n**Pattern:**\n```regex\n\\.\\.\\.  # Three consecutive dots in output\n```\n\n**Example:**\n```\nassert {'age': 25, '...name': 'Bob'} == {'age': 30, '...ame': 'Alice'}\n```\n\n### 3. Multiline String Escapes\n\nMultiline strings show escape sequences:\n\n**Pattern:**\n```regex\n\\\\n|\\\\t|\\\\r  # Escape sequences for newline, tab, carriage return\n```\n\n**Example:**\n```\nassert '\\n    This i...ontent.\\n    ' == '\\n    This i... lines.\\n    '\n```\n\n### 4. Platform-Specific Paths\n\nPath separators vary by platform:\n\n**Unix:**\n```\n/home/user/project/tests/test_file.py:15: in test_func\n```\n\n**Windows:**\n```\nC:\\Users\\project\\tests\\test_file.py:10: in test_func\n```\n\n**Solution:** Use `os.path.normpath()` for consistent path handling.\n\n### 5. Whitespace Variations\n\nDifferent traceback formats use different indentation:\n\n**--tb=short:** Indented with spaces\n```\nE     assert 'hello' == 'world'\nE       - world\n```\n\n**--tb=line:** No indentation\n```\nAssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\n```\n\n**--tb=no:** No indentation in summary\n```\nFAILED test_file.py::test_name - AssertionError\n```\n\n### 6. Test Execution Formats\n\n#### Verbose Format (`-v`, `-vv`)\n\n**Pattern:**\n```regex\n^\\s*(.+?)::(\\w+)\\s+(FAILED|PASSED|ERROR|SKIPPED)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]\n```\n\n**Example:**\n```\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py::test_simple_equality FAILED          [  6%]\n```\n\n#### Concise Format (no `-v`)\n\n**Pattern:**\n```regex\n^\\s*(.+?)\\s+([FPE\\.]+)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]\n```\n\n**Example:**\n```\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py FFFFFFFFFFFFFFF                       [100%]\n```\n\n## Section Markers\n\n### Failures Section\n\n**Regex:**\n```regex\n^===+ FAILURES ===+\n```\n\n### Summary Section\n\n**Regex:**\n```regex\n^===+ short test summary info ===+\n```\n\n### Session Start\n\n**Regex:**\n```regex\n^===+ test session starts ===+\n```\n\n### Session End\n\n**Regex:**\n```regex\n^===+ \\d+ (?:failed|passed) in [\\d.]+s ===+\n```\n\n## Comprehensive Pattern Reference\n\n### File Location Patterns\n\n| Pattern | Use Case | Format |\n|---------|----------|--------|\n| `^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):` | Generic file location | All formats |\n| `^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+in\\s+(\\w+)` | Short traceback | `--tb=short` |\n| `^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+AssertionError:\\s*(.+)` | Line traceback | `--tb=line` |\n\n### Assertion Patterns\n\n| Pattern | Use Case | Format |\n|---------|----------|--------|\n| `^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+)` | Generic assertion | All formats |\n| `^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+==\\s+(.+)` | Equality check | All formats |\n| `^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+in\\s+(.+)` | Membership test | All formats |\n| `^E?\\s*assert\\s+isinstance\\((.+?),\\s*(.+?)\\)` | Type check | All formats |\n\n### Diff Patterns\n\n| Pattern | Use Case | Format |\n|---------|----------|--------|\n| `^\\s*-\\s*(.+)` | Expected value marker | `--tb=short` |\n| `^\\s*\\+\\s*(.+)` | Actual value marker | `--tb=short` |\n| `^\\s*\\?\\s+(.+)` | Character position marker | `--tb=short` |\n| `^\\s*At\\s+index\\s+(\\d+)\\s+diff:\\s+(.+?)\\s+!=\\s+(.+)` | List/tuple diff | `--tb=short` |\n| `^\\s*Differing items:` | Dict diff header | `--tb=short` |\n| `^\\s*(?:E\\s+)?(Right\\|Left) contains (?:one more item\\|\\d+ more items?)\\s*:\\s*(.+)` | Range diff | `--tb=short` |\n| `^\\s*\\+\\s+where\\s+(.+?)\\s+=\\s+(.+)` | Type check clause | `--tb=short` |\n\n### Summary Patterns\n\n| Pattern | Use Case | Format |\n|---------|----------|--------|\n| `^(FAILED\\|PASSED\\|ERROR\\|SKIPPED)\\s+(.+?)::(\\w+)\\s+-\\s*(.+)` | Summary line | All formats |\n| `=\\s+(\\d+)\\s+(failed\\|passed\\|error\\|skipped)\\s+in\\s+([\\d.]+)s\\s+=` | Final count | All formats |\n\n## Testing and Verification\n\n### Verification Status\n\nAll patterns have been verified against real pytest output from:\n\n- **Pytest Version:** 8.3.3\n- **Python Version:** 3.12.8\n- **Test Coverage:** 15 assertion types\n- **Format Variations:** 3 core formats (`--tb=short`, `--tb=line`, `--tb=no`)\n\n### Verification Method\n\nPatterns were tested using:\n1. Manual regex testing against collected samples\n2. Automated verification script (`tools/verify_pytest_patterns.py`)\n3. Production parser implementation (`tools/parse_pytest_output.py`)\n\n### Verification Results\n\n- ✅ All core patterns verified\n- ✅ All assertion patterns verified\n- ✅ All diff patterns verified\n- ✅ Edge cases documented\n- ✅ Platform variations handled\n\n## Usage Recommendations\n\n### For Machine Parsing\n\n**Recommended format:** `--tb=line`\n- Single line per failure\n- Complete error message\n- Most parseable format\n\n**Alternative:** `-vv --tb=short`\n- Detailed assertion information\n- Complete diffs\n- More complex parsing\n\n### For Quick Status Checks\n\n**Recommended format:** `--tb=no`\n- Summary only\n- Fast to parse\n- No detailed information\n\n### For Detailed Analysis\n\n**Recommended format:** `-vv --tb=short`\n- Full assertion context\n- Character-level diffs\n- Index information for sequences\n- Hierarchical paths for nested structures\n\n## Implementation Notes\n\n### Pattern Order\n\nWhen parsing output, check patterns in this order:\n1. Section markers (to identify context)\n2. File location patterns (to identify failures)\n3. Assertion patterns (to classify assertion type)\n4. Diff patterns (to extract expected/actual values)\n\n### Whitespace Handling\n\nUse `\\s*` (zero or more whitespace) rather than `\\s+` (one or more) in patterns to handle format variations:\n\n```python\n# Good - handles both formats\nASSERT_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s*assert\\s+(.+)'\n\n# Less flexible - requires whitespace\nASSERT_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s+assert\\s+(.+)'\n```\n\n### Path Normalization\n\nAlways normalize file paths using `os.path.normpath()` to handle platform differences:\n\n```python\nimport os\n\nfile_path = match.group(1)  # From file location pattern\nnormalized_path = os.path.normpath(file_path)\n```\n\n## Related Documentation\n\n- **Parser Implementation:** `tools/parse_pytest_output.py`\n- **Pattern Verification:** `tools/pytest_pattern_verification.md`\n- **Command Patterns:** `docs/pytest-command-pattern.md`\n- **Parseability Verification:** `docs/bf-1rd15q-pytest-output-parseability-verification.md`\n\n## Change History\n\n| Version | Date | Changes |\n|---------|------|---------|\n| 1.0 | 2026-07-13 | Initial specification (bf-5x5xz1) |\n\n---\n\n**Specification Status:** ✅ COMPLETE  \n**Maintenance:** Update when pytest output format changes or new patterns are discovered  \n**Contact:** ARMOR Project\n"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"50e58331-b33d-40bc-9690-f4679b077403","uuid":"635a26da-87fa-4b96-b6af-eddccb7ca2f8"}

AssertionError\n99\t______________________________ test_dict_equality ______________________________\n100\t\n101\t    def test_dict_equality():\n102\t        \"\"\"Dictionary equality assertion failure.\"\"\"\n103\t        expected = {\"name\": \"Alice\", \"age\": 30, \"city\": \"NYC\"}\n104\t        actual = {\"name\": \"Bob\", \"age\": 25, \"city\": \"LA\"}\n105\t>       assert actual == expected, f\"Dictionaries don't match\"\n106\tE       AssertionError: Dictionaries don't match\n107\tE       assert {'age': 25, '...'name': 'Bob'} == {'age': 30, '...ame': 'Alice'}\n108\tE         \n109\tE         Differing items:\n110\tE         {'city': 'LA'} != {'city': 'NYC'}\n111\tE         {'age': 25} != {'age': 30}\n112\tE         {'name': 'Bob'} != {'name': 'Alice'}\n113\tE         \n114\tE         Full diff:...\n115\tE         \n116\tE         ...Full output truncated (14 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show\n117\t\n118\ttools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:24: diff.\"\"\"\n123\t        list1 = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]\n124\t        list2 = [1, 2, 10, 4, 5]\n125\t>       assert list1 == list2, \"Lists should be equal\"\n126\tE       AssertionError: Lists should be equal\n127\tE       assert [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] == [1, 2, 10, 4, 5]\n128\tE         \n129\tE         At index 2 diff: 3 != 10\n130\tE         \n131\tE         Full diff:\n132\tE           [\n133\tE               1,\n134\tE               2,...\n135\tE         \n136\tE         ...Full output truncated (7 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show\n137\t\n138\ttools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:31: AssertionError\n139\t```\n140\t\n141\t### Format 3: Single Line Format (`--tb=line`)\n142\t\n143\t**Flags:** `pytest --tb=line`  \n144\t**Characteristics:** One line per failure, most parseable, minimal context\n145\t\n146\t#### Sample Output\n147\t\n148\t```\n149\t=================================== FAILURES ===================================\n150\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\n151\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:24: AssertionError: Dictionaries don't match\n152\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:31: AssertionError: Lists should be equal\n153\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:44: AssertionError: assert '\\n    This i...ontent.\\n    ' == '\\n    This i... lines.\\n    '\n154\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:51: AssertionError: Numbers don't match: 5 != 10\n155\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:57: AssertionError: orange should be in the list\n156\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:64: AssertionError: Sequences should match\n157\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:81: AssertionError: Nested structures should match\n158\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:89: AssertionError: Floats don't match: 0.30000000000000004 != 0.3\n159\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:96: AssertionError: Both x and y should be True\n160\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:102: AssertionError: Uppercase conversion failed\n161\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:108: AssertionError: Value should be int, got str\n162\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:115: AssertionError: Sets should be equal\n163\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:122: AssertionError: Ranges should be equal\n164\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:129: AssertionError: Tuples should be equal\n165\t```\n166\t\n167\t## Common Patterns Across All Formats\n168\t\n169\t### 1. File Location Pattern\n170\t\n171\t**Regex:** `r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'`\n172\t\n173\t**Extracts:**\n174\t- Group 1: File path (e.g., `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py` or `/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py`)\n175\t- Group 2: Line number (e.g., `17`, `24`, `31`)\n176\t\n177\t**Works in all three formats:**\n178\t- Format 1: `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17:`\n179\t- Format 2: `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17:`\n180\t- Format 3: `/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17:`\n181\t\n182\t### 2. Test Name Pattern (Formats 1 & 2)\n183\t\n184\t**Regex:** `r'in\\s+(\\w+)'`\n185\t\n186\t**Extracts:**\n187\t- Group 1: Test function name (e.g., `test_simple_equality`, `test_dict_equality`)\n188\t\n189\t**Works in:**\n190\t- Format 1: `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: in test_simple_equality`\n191\t- Format 2: `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: in test_simple_equality`\n192\t- Format 3: Not available\n193\t\n194\t### 3. Error Message Pattern (Format 3)\n195\t\n196\t**Regex:** `r'AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'`\n197\t\n198\t**Extracts:**\n199\t- Group 1: Full error message (e.g., `Expected 'world', got 'hello'`)\n200\t\n201\t**Works in:**\n202\t- Format 3: `/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'`\n203\t- Formats 1 & 2: `E   AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'` (needs `E   ` prefix handling)\n204\t\n205\t## Assertion-Specific Patterns\n206\t\n207\t### Equality Assertion Pattern\n208\t\n209\t**Regex:** `r'assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+==\\s+(.+)'`\n210\t\n211\t**Extracts:**\n212\t- Group 1: Actual value (left side)\n213\t- Group 2: Expected value (right side)\n214\t\n215\t**Examples:**\n216\t- `assert 'hello' == 'world'` → actual=`'hello'`, expected=`'world'`\n217\t- `assert 5 == 10` → actual=`5`, expected=`10`\n218\t- `assert [1, 2, 3] == [1, 2, 10]` → actual=`[1, 2, 3]`, expected=`[1, 2, 10]`\n219\t\n220\t### Index Diff Pattern\n221\t\n222\t**Regex:** `r'At\\s+index\\s+(\\d+)\\s+diff:\\s+(.+?)\\s+!=\\s+(.+)'`\n223\t\n224\t**Extracts:**\n225\t- Group 1: Index position (e.g., `2`, `5`)\n226\t- Group 2: Actual value at index\n227\t- Group 3: Expected value at index\n228\t\n229\t**Examples:**\n230\t- `At index 2 diff: 3 != 10` → index=`2`, actual=`3`, expected=`10`\n231\t- `At index 5 diff: 10 != 5` → index=`5`, actual=`10`, expected=`5`\n232\t\n233\t### Differing Items Pattern\n234\t\n235\t**Regex:** `r'\\{(.+?)\\}\\s+!=\\s+\\{(.+?)\\}'`\n236\t\n237\t**Extracts:**\n238\t- Group 1: Left side (actual) key-value\n239\t- Group 2: Right side (expected) key-value\n240\t\n241\t**Examples:**\n242\t- `{'city': 'LA'} != {'city': 'NYC'}` → actual=`{'city': 'LA'}`, expected=`{'city': 'NYC'}`\n243\t- `{'age': 25} != {'age': 30}` → actual=`{'age': 25}`, expected=`{'age': 30}`\n244\t\n245\t### Set Operations Pattern\n246\t\n247\t**Left set regex:** `r'Extra items in the left set:'`  \n248\t**Right set regex:** `r'Extra items in the right set:'`\n249\t\n250\t**Followed by values on subsequent lines:**\n251\t- `4` (in left set)\n252\t- `5` (in right set)\n253\t\n254\t### Range Diff Pattern\n255\t\n256\t**Regex:** `r'(Right|Left) contains (?:one more item|\\d+ more items?)\\s*:\\s*(.+)'`\n257\t\n258\t**Extracts:**\n259\t- Group 1: Side (Right/Left)\n260\t- Group 2: Extra item value\n261\t\n262\t**Examples:**\n263\t- `Right contains one more item: 10` → side=`Right`, item=`10`\n264\t- `Left contains 2 more items: 3, 4` → side=`Left`, items=`3, 4`\n265\t\n266\t### Where Clause Pattern (Type Checks)\n267\t\n268\t**Regex:** `r'where\\s+(.+?)\\s+=\\s+(.+)'`\n269\t\n270\t**Extracts:**\n271\t- Group 1: Expression (e.g., `False`)\n272\t- Group 2: Computation (e.g., `isinstance('123', int)`)\n273\t\n274\t**Examples:**\n275\t- `where False = isinstance('123', int)` → expr=`False`, computation=`isinstance('123', int)`\n276\t\n277\t## Expected/Actual Extraction from Diffs\n278\t\n279\t### Diff Line Markers\n280\t\n281\t**Expected (minus) pattern:** `r'^\\s*-\\s*(.+)'`  \n282\t**Actual (plus) pattern:** `r'^\\s*\\+\\s*(.+)'`\n283\t\n284\t**Extracts:**\n285\t- Group 1: The value on that line\n286\t\n287\t**Examples:**\n288\t- `    - world` → `world`\n289\t- `    + hello` → `hello`\n290\t- `  -     'age': 30,` → `'age': 30,`\n291\t- `  +     'age': 25,` → `'age': 25,`\n292\t\n293\t### Position Markers\n294\t\n295\t**Regex:** `r'^\\s*\\?\\s+(.+)'`\n296\t\n297\t**Extracts:**\n298\t- Group 1: Position indicator (shows which characters differ)\n299\t\n300\t**Examples:**\n301\t- `?            ^^` → positions 2-3 differ\n302\t- `?              ^^^` → positions 3-5 differ\n303\t\n304\t## Edge Cases and Format Variations\n305\t\n306\t### 1. Truncated Output\n307\t\n308\t**Pattern:** `r'\\.\\.\\.Full output truncated\\s+\\((\\d+) lines hidden\\)'`  \n309\t**Occurs in:** Format 2 when using `-v` without `-vv`  \n310\t**Example:** `...Full output truncated (14 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show`\n311\t\n312\t### 2. Ellipsis in Values\n313\t\n314\t**Pattern:** Three consecutive dots in truncated values  \n315\t**Occurs in:** Format 2 and Format 3 for long values  \n316\t**Examples:**\n317\t- `assert '\\n    This i...ontent.\\n    ' == '\\n    This i... lines.\\n    '`\n318\t- `assert [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 10, ...] == [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, ...]`\n319\t\n320\t### 3. Multiline String Escapes\n321\t\n322\t**Pattern:** Escape sequences like `\\n`, `\\t` in string diffs  \n323\t**Occurs in:** All formats  \n324\t**Example:** `assert '\\n    This i...ontent.\\n    ' == '\\n    This i... lines.\\n    '`\n325\t\n326\t### 4. Path Format Variations\n327\t\n328\t**Relative paths** (Formats 1 & 2): `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17`  \n329\t**Absolute paths** (Format 3): `/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17`\n330\t\n331\t**Solution:** Use `os.path.normpath()` for consistent path handling.\n332\t\n333\t### 5. Whitespace Variations\n334\t\n335\t**Format 1 & 2:** `E   ` prefix (3 spaces after E)  \n336\t**Format 3:** No prefix, direct text\n337\t\n338\t**Pattern variations:**\n339\t- `E   AssertionError:` (Format 1 & 2)\n340\t- `AssertionError:` (Format 3)\n341\t- `E   assert` (Format 1 & 2)\n342\t- `assert` (implied in Format 3)\n343\t\n344\t### 6. Floating-Point Precision\n345\t\n346\t**Pattern:** Numbers like `0.30000000000000004` vs `0.3`  \n347\t**Occurs in:** All formats  \n348\t**Example:** `AssertionError: Floats don't match: 0.30000000000000004 != 0.3`\n349\t\n350\t### 7. Boolean Logic\n351\t\n352\t**Pattern:** Complex expressions without explicit expected/actual  \n353\t**Example:** `assert (True and False)`  \n354\t**Note:** No diff shown, just the assertion\n355\t\n356\t### 8. Nested Structure Truncation\n357\t\n358\t**Pattern:** Deep nesting gets truncated in Format 2  \n359\t**Example:** `assert {'users': [{'...92, 87, 91]}]} == {'users': [{'...92, 99, 91]}]}`  \n360\t**Note:** Use `-vv` for complete nested diffs\n361\t\n362\t## Recommended Parsing Strategy by Format\n363\t\n364\t### Format 1: Full Detailed (`-vv --tb=short`)\n365\t\n366\t**Parse order:**\n367\t1. Extract file:line using `r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'`\n368\t2. Extract test name using `r'in\\s+(\\w+)'`\n369\t3. Extract assertion using `r'assert\\s+(.+)'`\n370\t4. Classify assertion type (equality, contains, isinstance, etc.)\n371\t5. Extract diff lines using `r'^\\s*[-+]\\s*(.+)'`\n372\t6. Parse expected from `-` lines, actual from `+` lines\n373\t\n374\t### Format 2: Long Context (`-v --tb=long`)\n375\t\n376\t**Parse order:**\n377\t1. Extract file:line using `r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'`\n378\t2. Extract test name using `r'in\\s+(\\w+)'`\n379\t3. Look for `>` marker to find failing line\n380\t4. Parse assertion from line with `>`\n381\t5. Handle truncated output warnings\n382\t6. Extract available diffs (may be incomplete)\n383\t\n384\t### Format 3: Single Line (`--tb=line`)\n385\t\n386\t**Parse order:**\n387\t1. Extract file:line using `r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'`\n388\t2. Extract error message using `r'AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'`\n389\t3. Parse expected/actual from message if available (e.g., \"Expected X, got Y\")\n390\t4. No test name available in this format\n391\t\n392\t## Summary Table\n393\t\n394\t| Field | Format 1 | Format 2 | Format 3 | Pattern |\n395\t|-------|----------|----------|----------|---------|\n396\t| File path | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | `r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'` |\n397\t| Line number | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | Same as above |\n398\t| Test name | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | `r'in\\s+(\\w+)'` |\n399\t| Error message | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | `r'AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'` |\n400\t| Expected value | ✅ Yes | ⚠️ Partial | ❌ No | From `-` diff lines |\n401\t| Actual value | ✅ Yes | ⚠️ Partial | ❌ No | From `+` diff lines |\n402\t| Index diffs | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | `r'At\\s+index\\s+(\\d+)'` |\n403\t| Dict diffs | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | `r'Differing items:'` |\n404\t| Complete diffs | ✅ Yes | ⚠️ Truncated | ❌ No | N/A |\n405\t\n406\t## Conclusion\n407\t\n408\tThe three formats serve different purposes:\n409\t\n410\t- **Format 1 (`-vv --tb=short`)**: Best for detailed value extraction and debugging\n411\t- **Format 2 (`-v --tb=long`)**: Good for understanding code context, but diffs are truncated\n412\t- **Format 3 (`--tb=line`)**: Best for quick status checks and failure counting\n413\t\n414\tFor ARMOR's test failure analysis system, **Format 1** provides the most complete information for extracting file paths, line numbers, expected values, and actual values.\n415\t\n416\t---\n417\t\n418\t**Generated:** 2026-07-14 for ARMOR project (bead: bf-5x5xz1)  \n419\t**Parent Task:** bf-1ym8jw (Collect sample pytest failure outputs)  \n420\t**Source Samples:** Collected from actual pytest runs on Pytest Output Format Patterns\n\n**Task:** bf-5x5xz1 (Research and document pytest output formats)  \n**Date:** 2026-07-14  \n**Status:** ✅ Complete\n\n## Overview\n\nThis document analyzes the three primary pytest output formats collected from actual test runs and documents the exact patterns for extracting file paths, line numbers, expected values, and actual values.\n\n## The Three Sample Formats\n\nBased on the parent bead (bf-1ym8jw) sample collection, pytest produces three distinct output formats depending on flags:\n\n### Format 1: Full Detailed Format (`-vv --tb=short`)\n\n**Flags:** `pytest -vv --tb=short`  \n**Characteristics:** Complete diffs, multi-line assertions, detailed context\n\n#### Sample Output\n\n```\n_____________________________ lines hidden), use '-vv' to show\n\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:31: AssertionError\n```\n\n### Format 3: Single Line Format (`--tb=line`)\n\n**Flags:** `pytest --tb=line`  \n**Characteristics:** One line per failure, most parseable, minimal context\n\n#### Sample Output\n\n```\n=================================== FAILURES ===================================\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:24: AssertionError: Dictionaries don't match\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:31: AssertionError: Lists should be equal\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:44: AssertionError: assert '\\n    This i...ontent.\\n    ' == '\\n    This i... lines.\\n    '\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:51: AssertionError: Numbers don't match: 5 != 10\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:57: AssertionError: orange should be in the list\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:64: AssertionError: Sequences should
